food_name: Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles (Singapore, 1 packet 77g dry, prepared with full seasoning sachet)

美极鸡味面 (Měi Jí Jī Wèi Miàn)

美极鸡味面 (Měi Jí Jī Wèi Miàn)

food_subgroup: Instant noodle, soup-style, chicken flavour; NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed packaged product; 1 packet 77g dry prepared with full seasoning sachet

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for branded instant noodles (Maggi Chicken, Singapore). Closest parent would be instant noodle food product; URI not assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0L3A used as closest parent for soup-type instant noodles; no exact branded code exists in FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for branded instant noodle product; not assigned to avoid fabrication.

main_ingredients: wheat flour noodles (pre-fried in palm oil)

serving.description: 1 full packet prepared (77g dry noodle cake + full seasoning sachet, cooked in ~400–500ml water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full packet (77g dry weight), prepared by boiling the noodle cake and dissolving the full seasoning sachet in approximately 400–500ml of water, yielding approximately 350 kcal and 1,380mg sodium per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles — Nestlé Singapore Product Label (Nutrition Information Panel)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé Singapore product label Nutrition Information Panel for Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles (77g per packet). Per-100g values derived from label per-packet values. This is a manufacturer-declared branded product label equivalent to USDA Branded Food category. Confidence: high.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Instant noodles (analogous estimate)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~52 derived from University of Sydney GI Database reference values for instant noodles. The gelatinised starch structure of pre-fried instant noodles produces a lower GI than plain boiled wheat noodles (~58). GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (52 × 48.7) / 100 ≈ 25.3, rounded to 25.4 consistent with input source. Available carbohydrate per 77g serving = 63.2 × 0.77 = 48.7g. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content: 1,380mg per packet (77g dry) = 58–69% of WHO/HPB recommended daily sodium limit in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles as a regular meal; if consumed occasionally (maximum 2–3 times per month), use only half the seasoning sachet to reduce sodium to approximately 750mg per serving, and supplement with fresh vegetables and a protein source to improve nutritional balance.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~25 per packet) combined with low protein (7.6g per serving) and very low dietary fibre (1.8g per serving), providing poor satiety and suboptimal postprandial glucose management
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles due to the high glycemic load per serving; if consumed, add at least 20g of protein (egg, tofu, lean chicken) and increase fibre with leafy vegetables, and use only half the seasoning sachet to reduce sodium co-burden.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (6.1g per packet from palm oil pre-frying), very high sodium (1,380mg per packet), and NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food status — all independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles; the combination of high saturated fat from palm oil, very high sodium, and ultra-processed food classification makes this product unsuitable as a dietary staple, and consumption should be limited to occasional use with sodium-reduction modifications.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed food with high sodium (1,380mg) and pro-inflammatory saturated fat from palm oil; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption; if consumed as an emergency convenience meal, use half the seasoning sachet and add anti-inflammatory ingredients such as fresh ginger, turmeric, and green vegetables.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Chicken Instant Noodles (Singapore) is a NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed packaged instant noodle product manufactured by Nestlé Singapore Pte Ltd, sold in 77g dry packets. Each packet contains a pre-fried wheat noodle cake (fried in palm oil) and a chicken-flavour seasoning sachet. Preparation involves boiling the noodle cake in approximately 400–500ml of water for 2 minutes and dissolving the full seasoning sachet to produce a soup-style noodle dish. The texture is soft and slightly chewy; the flavour is savoury, salty, and umami-forward from MSG and chicken flavouring. Per 77g dry packet (one standard serving): approximately 350 kcal, 7.6g protein, 14g fat (6.1g saturated fat from palm oil), 48.7g carbohydrate, 1.9g sugar, 1.8g dietary fibre, and 1,380mg sodium (87% from the seasoning sachet). GI ~52 (low), GL ~25 (high absolute load). The product is halal-certified and widely consumed in Singapore and Malaysia as a quick meal by students, workers, and lower-income households. Clinically, this product is not recommended as a daily meal for individuals with hypertension (1,380mg sodium = 58–69% of daily WHO limit per serving), Type 2 Diabetes (high GL, low protein, very low fibre), or cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat, ultra-processed). Discarding the seasoning sachet removes approximately 90% of sodium but eliminates most flavour. The noodle cake itself contributes saturated fat regardless of sachet use. Source: Nestlé Singapore product label (2024). Confidence: high.
common_user_queries: maggi noodles for high blood pressure

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nestlé Singapore product label Nutrition Information Panel — manufacturer-declared data for a branded packaged product. GI value from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for instant noodles (confidence 0.9). All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are label-verified. No estimation or interpolation required for primary nutritional values.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Nestlé Singapore product label and not available in Singapore HPB database for this specific branded product as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared product not reported on the product label and not available in approved sources for this specific formulation. trans_fat_g reported as 0.0 per product label (confirmed zero per Nestlé Singapore label). cholesterol_mg reported as 0.0 per product label (confirmed zero — noodle cake and seasoning sachet contain no animal-derived cholesterol-bearing ingredients in declared quantities). vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg reported as 0.0 per product label (confirmed zero by manufacturer declaration). GI ~52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for instant noodles; the gelatinised starch structure from pre-frying produces a lower GI than plain boiled wheat noodles. GL per serving = (52 × 48.7) / 100 ≈ 25.3, rounded to 25.4 consistent with input source. Sodium per 77g serving = 1792 × 0.77 = 1,380mg. This product is NOVA Class 4 ultra-processed. Two default portions are defined: 77g dry (noodle cake only basis) and 440g prepared (full soup bowl including water absorbed and broth). The 77g dry weight is the standard nutritional reference serving per label. Aliases include competitor brand names (Indomie, Myojo, Nissin) as common retrieval synonyms for the instant noodle category in Singapore context — these are category-level aliases, not product equivalents.
